{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Executive",
  "description": "Hazibian Interalnation Limited is a future thinking real estate and construction firm, with the sole aim of bringing about realisation of dreams in real estate.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Executive

Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki - Ajah, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	We are seeking a results-driven Business Development Executive responsible for generating revenue, closing high-value deals, and expanding our client base across real estate, shortlet apartments, and agricultural investments (Zibian Farms and farm funding deals).
	This role is strictly for individuals who are target-oriented, persuasive, and financially motivated. If you are not comfortable with targets and performance-based rewards, this role is not for you.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify, engage, and convert high-quality leads into paying clients
	Sell and close deals across real estate, shortlet, and farm investment packages
	Build and maintain strong client relationships for repeat business and referrals
	Conduct presentations, site visits, and client consultations
	Develop strategic partnerships to drive business growth
	Follow up consistently on prospects and maintain a strong sales pipeline
	Meet and exceed monthly revenue targets


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a Bachelor&#39;s Degree with 2&ndash;5 years experience in sales or business development.
	Proven track record of closing deals and generating revenue
	Strong communication, negotiation, and persuasion skills
	Confidence in engaging high-net-worth or serious investors
	Ability to work independently and deliver results under pressure
	Good knowledge of Lagos market dynamics is an added advantage


Who Should Apply:


	Individuals who are hungry for financial growth
	Self-starters who take ownership and deliver results
	Professionals who understand that income is tied to performance


Compensation Structure


	Base Salary: N120,000 - N200,000 / Month (Competitive)
	High Commission on every closed deal
	Performance bonuses for exceeding targets
	Top performers in this role have the potential to earn N300,000 &ndash; N1,000,000+ monthly


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;admin@hazibianinternational.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Important Note:&nbsp;This is a performance-driven role. Only candidates who are confident in their ability to generate revenue and close deals should apply.",

  "validThrough": "2026-07-01",

      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },

  },
  "baseSalary": {
    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",

Business Development Executive Job at Hazibian Interalnation Limited




        
    

        
        Hazibian Interalnation Limited is a future thinking real estate and construction firm, with the sole aim of bringing about realisation of dreams in real estate.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Executive

Job Location: Lekki - Ajah, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a results-driven Business Development Executive responsible for generating revenue, closing high-value deals, and expanding our client base across real estate, shortlet apartments, and agricultural investments (Zibian Farms and farm funding deals).
	This role is strictly for individuals who are target-oriented, persuasive, and financially motivated. If you are not comfortable with targets and performance-based rewards, this role is not for you.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify, engage, and convert high-quality leads into paying clients
	Sell and close deals across real estate, shortlet, and farm investment packages
	Build and maintain strong client relationships for repeat business and referrals
	Conduct presentations, site visits, and client consultations
	Develop strategic partnerships to drive business growth
	Follow up consistently on prospects and maintain a strong sales pipeline
	Meet and exceed monthly revenue targets


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a Bachelor's Degree with 2–5 years experience in sales or business development.
	Proven track record of closing deals and generating revenue
	Strong communication, negotiation, and persuasion skills
	Confidence in engaging high-net-worth or serious investors
	Ability to work independently and deliver results under pressure
	Good knowledge of Lagos market dynamics is an added advantage


Who Should Apply:


	Individuals who are hungry for financial growth
	Self-starters who take ownership and deliver results
	Professionals who understand that income is tied to performance


Compensation Structure


	Base Salary: N120,000 - N200,000 / Month (Competitive)
	High Commission on every closed deal
	Performance bonuses for exceeding targets
	Top performers in this role have the potential to earn N300,000 – N1,000,000+ monthly


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Important Note: This is a performance-driven role. Only candidates who are confident in their ability to generate revenue and close deals should apply.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Internal Audit Officer",
  "description": "FairMoney is a pioneering mobile banking institution specializing in extending credit to emerging markets. Established in 2017, the company currently operates primarily within Nigeria, and it has secured nearly &euro;50 million in funding from renowned global investors, including Tiger Global, DST, and Flourish Ventures.

In alignment with its vision, FairMoney is actively constructing the foremost mobile banking platform and point-of-sale (POS) solution tailored for emerging markets. The journey began with the introduction of a digital microcredit application exclusively available on Android and iOS devices. Today, FairMoney has significantly expanded its range of services, encompassing a comprehensive suite of financial products, such as current accounts, savings accounts, debit cards, and state-of-the-art POS solutions designed to meet the needs of both merchants and agents.

To gain deeper insights into FairMoney&#39;s pivotal role in reshaping Africa&#39;s financial landscape, we invite you to watch this informative video.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Title&nbsp;Internal Audit Officer

Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time&nbsp;

About This Role


	As an Internal Audit Officer, you will play a critical role in supporting the internal audit function, ensuring the organization&#39;s operational and financial processes align with established standards and best practices.
	You will work closely with the Head of Internal Audit to assess risks, enhance controls, and provide valuable insights for decision-making.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop detailed audit work programs and comprehensive audit planning memoranda for each audit engagement
	Perform audit tests of key operational and financial processes, ensuring compliance with professional standards and methodology
	Prepare and maintain accurate working papers in alignment with established audit standards
	Prepare and present accurate internal audit reports for review by the Head of Internal Audit
	Conduct prepayment checks on transactions to ensure value for money on all transactions and contracts
	Update the audit tracker based on feedback obtained from process owners
	Assist the Head of Internal Audit in the development of the Annual Audit Plan
	Provide support to the Head of Internal Audit in preparing presentations and reports for the Board
	Stay updated on emerging trends and developments in auditing practices, integrating best practices into audit processes
	Perform additional tasks and responsibilities as assigned by the Head of Internal Audit


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related numerate discipline
	Minimum of 3 years in internal audit, with at least 1 year in the financial services sector.
	ACA, ACCA, CIA, or CISA certification is required
	Proven experience in auditing, with a strong understanding of audit methodologies and risk management practices
	Knowledge of internal control frameworks (e.g., COSO)
	Exceptional communication skills, analytical and problem-solving abilities to identify and resolve issues effectively
	Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite, including Excel, Word, and PowerPoint
	High ethical standards and professionalism, with the ability to handle confidential information sensitively and discretely
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively as part of a team, with a proactive approach to identifying and addressing control weaknesses.


Benefits


	Private Health Insurance
	Pension Plan
	Paid Time Off
	Hybrid work
	Training &amp; Development programs


Recruitment Process


	Screening with a member of the recruitment team (30 min)
	Take Home Assignment
	Technical Interview with the Lead - Internal Audit min)
	Final Interview with Managing Director
",
  "datePosted": "2026-04-29",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-30",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

  },

Internal Audit Officer Job at FairMoney




        
    

        
        FairMoney is a pioneering mobile banking institution specializing in extending credit to emerging markets. Established in 2017, the company currently operates primarily within Nigeria, and it has secured nearly €50 million in funding from renowned global investors, including Tiger Global, DST, and Flourish Ventures.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



In alignment with its vision, FairMoney is actively constructing the foremost mobile banking platform and point-of-sale (POS) solution tailored for emerging markets. The journey began with the introduction of a digital microcredit application exclusively available on Android and iOS devices. Today, FairMoney has significantly expanded its range of services, encompassing a comprehensive suite of financial products, such as current accounts, savings accounts, debit cards, and state-of-the-art POS solutions designed to meet the needs of both merchants and agents.

To gain deeper insights into FairMoney's pivotal role in reshaping Africa's financial landscape, we invite you to watch this informative video.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Title Internal Audit Officer

Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time 

About This Role


	As an Internal Audit Officer, you will play a critical role in supporting the internal audit function, ensuring the organization's operational and financial processes align with established standards and best practices.
	You will work closely with the Head of Internal Audit to assess risks, enhance controls, and provide valuable insights for decision-making.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop detailed audit work programs and comprehensive audit planning memoranda for each audit engagement
	Perform audit tests of key operational and financial processes, ensuring compliance with professional standards and methodology
	Prepare and maintain accurate working papers in alignment with established audit standards
	Prepare and present accurate internal audit reports for review by the Head of Internal Audit
	Conduct prepayment checks on transactions to ensure value for money on all transactions and contracts
	Update the audit tracker based on feedback obtained from process owners
	Assist the Head of Internal Audit in the development of the Annual Audit Plan
	Provide support to the Head of Internal Audit in preparing presentations and reports for the Board
	Stay updated on emerging trends and developments in auditing practices, integrating best practices into audit processes
	Perform additional tasks and responsibilities as assigned by the Head of Internal Audit


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related numerate discipline
	Minimum of 3 years in internal audit, with at least 1 year in the financial services sector.
	ACA, ACCA, CIA, or CISA certification is required
	Proven experience in auditing, with a strong understanding of audit methodologies and risk management practices
	Knowledge of internal control frameworks (e.g., COSO)
	Exceptional communication skills, analytical and problem-solving abilities to identify and resolve issues effectively
	Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite, including Excel, Word, and PowerPoint
	High ethical standards and professionalism, with the ability to handle confidential information sensitively and discretely
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively as part of a team, with a proactive approach to identifying and addressing control weaknesses.


Benefits


	Private Health Insurance
	Pension Plan
	Paid Time Off
	Hybrid work
	Training & Development programs


Recruitment Process


	Screening with a member of the recruitment team (30 min)
	Take Home Assignment
	Technical Interview with the Lead - Internal Audit min)
	Final Interview with Managing Director
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Project Manager",
  "description": "At Tetra Pak, we commit to making food safe and available, everywhere, and we protect what&#39;s good - protecting food, protecting people and protecting the planet. By doing so we touch millions of people&#39;s lives every day. And we need people like you to make it happen.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Project Manager
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Job Summary


	We are looking for an experienced project manager who strongly contributes to the overall successful management of multidiscipline teams and ensure each project phase is completed on time, within budget and to the customers&rsquo; satisfaction.
	The ideal candidate will focus on accomplishing the targets of the different projects in the market, developing the project team to improve team performance by building team cohesiveness, training, and motivating in order to facilitate cooperation, ensure project efficiency and boost morale.
	All members of the project team as well as other areas within the company or individuals influence the project&rsquo;s results or execution (project stakeholders), which are identified and managed as a continuous process during the entire project lifetime, in order to understand their degree of influence and to manage their expectations.
	The level and the nature of the interactions can vary according to the stakeholders and their level of influence, as defined in the project communication plan. Overall responsibility will be for projects type L1-L3 (processing project applicant level) and/or L1-L3-NPI (packaging project applicant level) in all respects i.e. cost, time, quality, and scope of obligations.
	The successful candidate will report to the Manager Project Management &amp; Implementation - Maghreb &amp; West Africa based in the Nigeria.
	The position will be office based in Lagos, Nigeria but requires traveling to customer site maximum 20-30% yearly to do kit installation and support work.


What You Will Do


	Integrate the multiple related activities (and/or sub projects) to deliver the overall project scope and objectives.
	Define, communicate, and manage project scope, objectives and deliverables based on stakeholders&rsquo; requirement.
	Define a deliverable oriented work breakdown task.
	Develop, execute, control, and communicate the project plan.
	Define, communicate, manage and control the project budget.
	Define, plan, and manage systematic quality activities to meet project value through employing necessary processes, methods and tools.
	Create and lead the project organization based on project deliverables and define roles, responsibilities, and objectives.
	Develop and implement a communication plan based on stakeholders needs and expectations.
	Develop, implement a monitor the risk management plan.
	Create and drive the project procurement plan to ensure the supplier selection and management fulfill project objectives.
	Manage contractual arrangements with all necessary organizational entities and maintains the support and engagement of the stakeholder group involved.
	Overall responsible for project execution and provide project leadership to deliver project result.
	Secure project deliverables according to project agreement (contract) and act according to responsibilities in the project charter.
	Breakdown and plan the project into phases and fulfil the plan and project milestones in time and in full according to budget.
	Drive productivity in project through utilization of global reusable solutions, processes, and tools (OFCE, project governance, risk management, etc.).
	Manage scope changes and issue variation orders (VO&acute;s).
	Provide lesson learned and providing input to improvement or development of processes and tools.


Profile Description
We believe you have:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in engineering (Mechanical, Industrial, Chemical, and other related fields) is preferred.
	Minimum 5 years of experience as a Project Manager or relevant experience handling projects, dealing with different stakeholders, actively participation in projects, leading technical teams and executing with a high sense of integration within different areas in the organization.
	Fluent French, Proficiency in English, both written and spoken, is required.
	Previous project management experience preferred. You are capable to manage complex stakeholders both internal and external at all levels making use of unformal authority and lead teams without authority.
	Capacity to deal with the uncertainty in complex projects.
	You are capable and used to apply professional project management competences according to PMI standards.
	Project Management Professional (PMP)&reg; Certification is preferred.
	Strong computer skills required (proficiency with excel and spreadsheets required).
	Self-motivated, results oriented, high sense of discipline, attention to details, ability to motivate a team, high level of negotiation skills, creative and adaptable.
	Ability to handle multiple stakeholders with a customer focused mindset
	Results-oriented with strong analytical skills.

Project Manager Job at Tetra Pak Nigeria




        
    

        
        At Tetra Pak, we commit to making food safe and available, everywhere, and we protect what's good - protecting food, protecting people and protecting the planet. By doing so we touch millions of people's lives every day. And we need people like you to make it happen.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Manager
Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for an experienced project manager who strongly contributes to the overall successful management of multidiscipline teams and ensure each project phase is completed on time, within budget and to the customers’ satisfaction.
	The ideal candidate will focus on accomplishing the targets of the different projects in the market, developing the project team to improve team performance by building team cohesiveness, training, and motivating in order to facilitate cooperation, ensure project efficiency and boost morale.
	All members of the project team as well as other areas within the company or individuals influence the project’s results or execution (project stakeholders), which are identified and managed as a continuous process during the entire project lifetime, in order to understand their degree of influence and to manage their expectations.
	The level and the nature of the interactions can vary according to the stakeholders and their level of influence, as defined in the project communication plan. Overall responsibility will be for projects type L1-L3 (processing project applicant level) and/or L1-L3-NPI (packaging project applicant level) in all respects i.e. cost, time, quality, and scope of obligations.
	The successful candidate will report to the Manager Project Management & Implementation - Maghreb & West Africa based in the Nigeria.
	The position will be office based in Lagos, Nigeria but requires traveling to customer site maximum 20-30% yearly to do kit installation and support work.


What You Will Do


	Integrate the multiple related activities (and/or sub projects) to deliver the overall project scope and objectives.
	Define, communicate, and manage project scope, objectives and deliverables based on stakeholders’ requirement.
	Define a deliverable oriented work breakdown task.
	Develop, execute, control, and communicate the project plan.
	Define, communicate, manage and control the project budget.
	Define, plan, and manage systematic quality activities to meet project value through employing necessary processes, methods and tools.
	Create and lead the project organization based on project deliverables and define roles, responsibilities, and objectives.
	Develop and implement a communication plan based on stakeholders needs and expectations.
	Develop, implement a monitor the risk management plan.
	Create and drive the project procurement plan to ensure the supplier selection and management fulfill project objectives.
	Manage contractual arrangements with all necessary organizational entities and maintains the support and engagement of the stakeholder group involved.
	Overall responsible for project execution and provide project leadership to deliver project result.
	Secure project deliverables according to project agreement (contract) and act according to responsibilities in the project charter.
	Breakdown and plan the project into phases and fulfil the plan and project milestones in time and in full according to budget.
	Drive productivity in project through utilization of global reusable solutions, processes, and tools (OFCE, project governance, risk management, etc.).
	Manage scope changes and issue variation orders (VO´s).
	Provide lesson learned and providing input to improvement or development of processes and tools.


Profile Description
We believe you have:


	Bachelor’s degree in engineering (Mechanical, Industrial, Chemical, and other related fields) is preferred.
	Minimum 5 years of experience as a Project Manager or relevant experience handling projects, dealing with different stakeholders, actively participation in projects, leading technical teams and executing with a high sense of integration within different areas in the organization.
	Fluent French, Proficiency in English, both written and spoken, is required.
	Previous project management experience preferred. You are capable to manage complex stakeholders both internal and external at all levels making use of unformal authority and lead teams without authority.
	Capacity to deal with the uncertainty in complex projects.
	You are capable and used to apply professional project management competences according to PMI standards.
	Project Management Professional (PMP)® Certification is preferred.
	Strong computer skills required (proficiency with excel and spreadsheets required).
	Self-motivated, results oriented, high sense of discipline, attention to details, ability to motivate a team, high level of negotiation skills, creative and adaptable.
	Ability to handle multiple stakeholders with a customer focused mindset
	Results-oriented with strong analytical skills.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Account Executive",
  "description": "Watolex Crown Integrated Service is a premier human resources consulting firm dedicated to providing comprehensive solutions in recruitment, capacity building, and management consultancy. Our mission is to meet the evolving needs of our clients through unwavering professionalism and exceptional customer service. Driven by a pursuit of excellence, we strive to enhance every aspect of our operations, ensuring optimal outcomes for our clients.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Executive

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Account Executive.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing financial records, supporting day-to-day accounting operations, and ensuring compliance with established financial procedures.
	This role requires a strong understanding of accounting principles, accuracy, and the ability to work in a fast-paced environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate financial records and ensure proper documentation of all transactions
	Prepare and process invoices, payments, and expense reports
	Reconcile bank statements and monitor company accounts
	Assist in the preparation of financial reports and statements
	Support budgeting and forecasting activities
	Ensure compliance with internal controls, policies, and regulatory requirements
	Handle accounts payable and receivable functions
	Liaise with internal teams and external stakeholders on financial matters
	Assist with audits and provide required documentation when necessary


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field
	1&ndash;5 years of relevant accounting experience
	Strong knowledge of basic accounting principles and practices
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software
	Professional certification (e.g., ICAN, ACCA) or in progress is an added advantage
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail
	Good analytical and problem-solving skills
	Strong communication and organizational skills
	Ability to work independently and meet deadlines


Salary
N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to:&nbsp;infowatolexcis@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Account Executive Job at Watolex Crown Integrated Service




        
    

        
        Watolex Crown Integrated Service is a premier human resources consulting firm dedicated to providing comprehensive solutions in recruitment, capacity building, and management consultancy. Our mission is to meet the evolving needs of our clients through unwavering professionalism and exceptional customer service. Driven by a pursuit of excellence, we strive to enhance every aspect of our operations, ensuring optimal outcomes for our clients.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Executive

Job Location: Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Account Executive.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing financial records, supporting day-to-day accounting operations, and ensuring compliance with established financial procedures.
	This role requires a strong understanding of accounting principles, accuracy, and the ability to work in a fast-paced environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate financial records and ensure proper documentation of all transactions
	Prepare and process invoices, payments, and expense reports
	Reconcile bank statements and monitor company accounts
	Assist in the preparation of financial reports and statements
	Support budgeting and forecasting activities
	Ensure compliance with internal controls, policies, and regulatory requirements
	Handle accounts payable and receivable functions
	Liaise with internal teams and external stakeholders on financial matters
	Assist with audits and provide required documentation when necessary


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field
	1–5 years of relevant accounting experience
	Strong knowledge of basic accounting principles and practices
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software
	Professional certification (e.g., ICAN, ACCA) or in progress is an added advantage
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail
	Good analytical and problem-solving skills
	Strong communication and organizational skills
	Ability to work independently and meet deadlines


Salary
N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Finance Manager",
  "description": "Izy Global Partners LLP is a premium legal advisory firm serving Ultra High Net Worth individuals, international aviation finance counterparties, and senior corporate executives. We operate across five service lines; Aviation Finance, Real Estate, Financing, Private Wealth, and Legal &amp; Compliance Advisory. We are at the founding stage and building a team of professionals who match the standard of work we deliver.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)

Job Summary


	The Finance Manager owns the firm&#39;s financial operations end-to-end, from day-to-day bookkeeping and client invoicing to monthly management accounts, payroll, tax filings, and direct financial reporting to the Founder.
	You will manage operating, USD, client trust, and reserve accounts, and work alongside our external ICAN-certified accountants on annual returns and statutory filings.
	This is a hands-on, sole-finance-person role at this stage. The firm&#39;s financial integrity rests with you.


Responsibilities


	Issuing client invoices in NGN and USD via relevant softwares, in compliance with VAT requirements
	Managing the client trust account in line with AML policy and NBA Rules of Professional Conduct
	Monthly reconciliation of all bank accounts (operating, USD, trust, reserve)
	Processing monthly payroll, PAYE, pension, and HMO premiums via relevant softwares
	Filing monthly PAYE returns to FCT-IRS and VAT returns to FIRS
	Maintaining WHT credit note records - issuing to clients, collecting from vendors
	Preparing monthly management accounts (P&amp;L, cash flow, key metrics) for the Founder
	Preparing the annual budget and tracking actuals against it
	Maintaining the chart of accounts and financial records to audit-ready standard


Requirements


	Minimum 3 years of finance or accounting experience in Nigeria
	ICAN qualification, or demonstrably on track toward ICAN membership
	Strong working knowledge of Nigerian tax obligations: VAT, WHT, PAYE, Pension Reform Act 2014
	Practical fluency in Zoho Books or QuickBooks; experience with Nigerian payroll systems is an advantage
	High numerical accuracy, attention to detail, and the discipline to reconcile cleanly every month


Strongly preferred:


	Prior experience in a law firm, financial services, or premium professional services environment
	Direct experience managing a client trust account
	Experience preparing management accounts in a multi-currency context.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested candidates should send their&nbsp; CV and brief cover note to:&nbsp;office@izyglobalpartners.com&nbsp;using &ldquo;Finance Manager&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Finance Manager Job at IZY Group Limited




        
    

        
        Izy Global Partners LLP is a premium legal advisory firm serving Ultra High Net Worth individuals, international aviation finance counterparties, and senior corporate executives. We operate across five service lines; Aviation Finance, Real Estate, Financing, Private Wealth, and Legal & Compliance Advisory. We are at the founding stage and building a team of professionals who match the standard of work we deliver.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Manager

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Finance Manager owns the firm's financial operations end-to-end, from day-to-day bookkeeping and client invoicing to monthly management accounts, payroll, tax filings, and direct financial reporting to the Founder.
	You will manage operating, USD, client trust, and reserve accounts, and work alongside our external ICAN-certified accountants on annual returns and statutory filings.
	This is a hands-on, sole-finance-person role at this stage. The firm's financial integrity rests with you.


Responsibilities


	Issuing client invoices in NGN and USD via relevant softwares, in compliance with VAT requirements
	Managing the client trust account in line with AML policy and NBA Rules of Professional Conduct
	Monthly reconciliation of all bank accounts (operating, USD, trust, reserve)
	Processing monthly payroll, PAYE, pension, and HMO premiums via relevant softwares
	Filing monthly PAYE returns to FCT-IRS and VAT returns to FIRS
	Maintaining WHT credit note records - issuing to clients, collecting from vendors
	Preparing monthly management accounts (P&L, cash flow, key metrics) for the Founder
	Preparing the annual budget and tracking actuals against it
	Maintaining the chart of accounts and financial records to audit-ready standard


Requirements


	Minimum 3 years of finance or accounting experience in Nigeria
	ICAN qualification, or demonstrably on track toward ICAN membership
	Strong working knowledge of Nigerian tax obligations: VAT, WHT, PAYE, Pension Reform Act 2014
	Practical fluency in Zoho Books or QuickBooks; experience with Nigerian payroll systems is an advantage
	High numerical accuracy, attention to detail, and the discipline to reconcile cleanly every month


Strongly preferred:


	Prior experience in a law firm, financial services, or premium professional services environment
	Direct experience managing a client trust account
	Experience preparing management accounts in a multi-currency context.


Method of Application 
Interested candidates should send their  CV and brief cover note to: [email protected] using “Finance Manager" as the subject of the email.
